vehicles.slot: Cap near selector base on console
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
This vehicle has a Lineartronic CVT — shift lock slot location: Cap near selector base on console.
Insert a thin key or small flathead into the slot.
Press firmly and hold.
Press the brake pedal while holding.
Move the selector to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to N normally.
FLATBED ONLY — Subaru Symmetrical AWD cannot be disabled. No 2WD mode exists on any trim.
PHEV/hybrid variants: 12V auxiliary only — never touch the HV battery.
vehicles.notes: AWD standard (Symmetrical). Manual: clutch to N.
vehicles.towMethod: FLATBED ONLY — Subaru Symmetrical AWD cannot be disabled on any model. If no flatbed available, use wheel-lift with dollies on free-rolling axle.
towNotes: HYBRID/PHEV: Use 12V auxiliary battery ONLY for jumping. NEVER access the high-voltage battery pack.
FULL-TIME AWD: Cannot disengage AWD. Flatbed is the safest method. If using wheel-lift, dollies MUST be used on the free-rolling axle to prevent driveline damage.
MANUAL TRANSMISSION OPTION: If equipped with manual trans, no shift lock — depress clutch and select Neutral. No override slot needed.
CVT EQUIPPED: Do NOT exceed 35 mph or 50 miles with drive wheels on ground. Flatbed strongly preferred for CVTs.

knownIssues.title: Symmetrical AWD Cannot Be Disabled — Flatbed Only
knownIssues.detail: ALL Subaru Outback and Legacy models have Symmetrical AWD. There is no FWD trim, no 2WD mode, no transfer case to shift. FLATBED ONLY on every Outback and Legacy. Operators who attempt wheel-lift towing will immediately damage the center differential and AWD system.

battery.jumpTerminals: Under hood — standard terminals. XT PHEV: 12V only.
battery.warnings: Symmetrical AWD — flatbed only on ALL Outback/Legacy. No exceptions. PHEV: 12V only.

This vehicle has a Lineartronic CVT — shift lock slot location: Cap near selector base.
Insert a thin key or small flathead into the slot.
Press firmly and hold.
Press the brake pedal while holding.
Move the selector to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to N normally.
FLATBED ONLY — Subaru Symmetrical AWD cannot be disabled. No 2WD mode exists on any trim.
PHEV/hybrid variants: 12V auxiliary only — never touch the HV battery.
vehicles.notes: AWD standard. Manual: clutch to N.
vehicles.towMethod: FLATBED ONLY — Subaru Symmetrical AWD cannot be disabled. If no flatbed available, use wheel-lift with dollies on free-rolling axle.
towNotes: FULL-TIME AWD: Cannot disengage AWD. Flatbed is the safest method. If using wheel-lift, dollies MUST be used on the free-rolling axle to prevent driveline damage.
MANUAL TRANSMISSION OPTION: If equipped with manual trans, no shift lock — depress clutch and select Neutral. No override slot needed.
CVT EQUIPPED: Do NOT exceed 35 mph or 50 miles with drive wheels on ground. Flatbed strongly preferred for CVTs.

knownIssues.title: Symmetrical AWD Cannot Be Disabled — Flatbed Only
knownIssues.detail: ALL Forester models have Symmetrical AWD. No exceptions. FLATBED ONLY. Do not attempt wheel-lift under any circumstances.

vehicles.trans: CVT, 6-speed auto, manual, or PHEV eCVT
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
Locate the shift lock slot: Cap near selector base.
Insert a thin key or flathead into the slot and press firmly down.
While holding the slot, press the brake pedal.
Move the selector to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to N normally.
If using slot without power, apply consistent firm pressure — hybrid selectors may require more force.
Hybrid: 12V auxiliary only — never touch HV battery.
vehicles.towMethod: FLATBED ONLY — Subaru Symmetrical AWD cannot be disabled. Crosstrek PHEV: 12V only. If no flatbed available, use wheel-lift with dollies on free-rolling axle.
towNotes: HYBRID/PHEV: Use 12V auxiliary battery ONLY for jumping. NEVER access the high-voltage battery pack.
FULL-TIME AWD: Cannot disengage AWD. Flatbed is the safest method. If using wheel-lift, dollies MUST be used on the free-rolling axle to prevent driveline damage.
MANUAL TRANSMISSION OPTION: If equipped with manual trans, no shift lock — depress clutch and select Neutral. No override slot needed.

knownIssues.title: Symmetrical AWD Cannot Be Disabled — Flatbed Only
knownIssues.detail: ALL Crosstrek models have Symmetrical AWD. No exceptions. FLATBED ONLY. Crosstrek PHEV additionally has a high-voltage battery — 12V auxiliary only for jumping.

battery.jumpTerminals: Under hood — standard terminals. PHEV: 12V only.
battery.warnings: Symmetrical AWD — flatbed only. PHEV: 12V only.

shift: Turn ignition to ON.
This vehicle has a Lineartronic CVT — shift lock slot location: Cap near selector base.
Insert a thin key or small flathead into the slot.
Press firmly and hold.
Press the brake pedal while holding.
Move the selector to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to N normally.
CVT transmissions: do NOT exceed 35 mph or 50 miles with driven wheels on ground — flatbed strongly preferred.
vehicles.notes: STI: manual only — clutch to N. AWD standard.
vehicles.towMethod: FLATBED ONLY — Subaru Symmetrical AWD cannot be disabled. STI: manual only. If no flatbed available, use wheel-lift with dollies on free-rolling axle.
towNotes: FULL-TIME AWD: Cannot disengage AWD. Flatbed is the safest method. If using wheel-lift, dollies MUST be used on the free-rolling axle to prevent driveline damage.
MANUAL TRANSMISSION OPTION: If equipped with manual trans, no shift lock — depress clutch and select Neutral. No override slot needed.
CVT EQUIPPED: Do NOT exceed 35 mph or 50 miles with drive wheels on ground. Flatbed strongly preferred for CVTs.

knownIssues.title: Symmetrical AWD Cannot Be Disabled — Flatbed Only
knownIssues.detail: All Impreza, WRX, and STI models have Symmetrical AWD. FLATBED ONLY.

knownIssues.title: STI Is Manual Transmission — No Shift Lock Needed
knownIssues.detail: The STI uses a 6-speed manual transmission. Simply depress the clutch and select neutral. No shift lock release procedure is needed.

battery.warnings: Symmetrical AWD — flatbed only. STI: manual trans — no shift lock needed.

vehicles.slot: Cap near selector base on console (auto only)
shift: Turn ignition to ON (do not start engine).
Locate the shift lock release: Cap near selector base on console (auto only).
Pry off the cap or cover if present using your ignition key or a small flathead.
Insert the key (or a thin flathead) into the slot and press firmly downward.
While holding the slot, press the brake pedal.
Move the selector from Park to Neutral.
Remove key from slot and replace cap.
neutral: Apply parking brake and chock wheels.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood, driver side.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to Neutral normally.
If power cannot be restored, use the shift lock slot above — Cap near selector base on console (auto only).
vehicles.notes: Manual: clutch to N. RWD. Shared platform with Toyota GR86.
vehicles.towMethod: Lift from rear — rear (drive) wheels in the air or flatbed. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: RWD: Lift from REAR. Rear (drive) wheels are lifted in the air. If rear-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the REAR wheels and tow front-first.
LOW CLEARANCE VEHICLE: Use flatbed. Check ground clearance before attempting any wheel-lift — front splitter/rear diffuser damage is common.
MANUAL TRANSMISSION OPTION: If equipped with manual trans, no shift lock — depress clutch and select Neutral. No override slot needed.

vehicles.slot: Electronic selector — slot near selector base
shift: Turn the Subaru Solterra to ON/Ready mode.
Shift lock slot: Electronic selector — slot near selector base.
Insert a thin key or flathead into the slot and press firmly.
Press the brake pedal while holding the slot.
Move selector to N.
FLATBED ONLY — do not tow with driven wheels on the ground.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood (12V auxiliary). 12V AUXILIARY BATTERY ONLY — never touch the high-voltage battery pack.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to N normally.
FLATBED ONLY.
towNotes: ELECTRIC VEHICLE: FLATBED ONLY — never tow with any driven wheels on the ground. Regenerative braking system can damage motor/inverter.
If vehicle must be moved short distance without flatbed, use dollies under ALL driven wheels.
12V battery ONLY for jumping — NEVER touch or access the high-voltage (HV) system. HV components are marked with ORANGE cables/covers.

battery.warnings: NEVER touch HV battery. Flatbed only.
